title: Enforce PR-gated CI on main (protect branch)
description: Root cause of how bloat accumulated silently: direct pushes to main bypassed the size check. Ensure branch protection requires the build+size-check status before any push/merge to main succeeds, including admin overrides being visible.
design: GitHub repo settings change; no code. Must be done by repo admin.
acceptance_criteria:
1) GitHub branch protection on main requires status check 'build' (the job containing the size check) to pass.
2) 'Include administrators' enabled so admin pushes are also blocked.
3) Documented in CLAUDE.md or a CONTRIBUTING note: 'all changes via PR, no direct pushes'.
4) Verify by attempting a direct push that would exceed limit — should be rejected.
notes: Complementary control — bd memory blog-always-open-prs already documents the intent; this enforces it at the server.

title: Fix build asymmetry: npm run build must equal CI output
description: Currently npm run build produces 82KB CSS (no purgecss), while build:prod produces 67KB. Developers can't reproduce CI output locally, and the size check never fires against the dev build. Fix: set NODE_ENV=production in css:website script so purgecss always runs. Keep dev:css (watch mode) unpurged for fast iteration; only the one-shot build pipelines get purge.
design: Option A: set NODE_ENV=production in css:website script via cross-env. Option B: always enable purgecss in postcss.config.js and rely on Tailwind's own purge for dev (skipping the postcss pass). Preferred: A — explicit, matches existing build:prod pattern.
acceptance_criteria:
1) 'npm run build' and 'npm run build:prod' produce byte-identical _site/css/style.css.
2) 'npm run dev' / 'npm run dev:css' still works without purgecss (watch mode speed).
3) tests/unit/css/bundle-size.test.js passes after 'npm run build' alone.
4) CI unchanged (still uses build:prod, still passes).
notes: Gating dependency for the trim PRs: without this, local test feedback lies.

title: Add Playwright visual-regression baselines for CSS-sensitive pages
description: Before any CSS trim lands, capture baseline screenshots for pages that exercise the most CSS surfaces. Each subsequent trim PR must pass visual diff on these. Covers prose-heavy blog post, home, dictionary, diceware, and a post with TOC+mermaid+code blocks.
design: Use Playwright's built-in toHaveScreenshot matcher. Disable animations (reading-progress, transitions) via CSS inject before screenshot. Mask dynamic content (dates, reading-time) with selectors.
acceptance_criteria:
1) tests/e2e/visual-regression.spec.js (or extension to existing) captures and compares screenshots for: /, /en-us/gru-kms-windows/ (prose + code + internal links), /en-us/tools/diceware/ (shoelace + forms), /en-us/dictionary/, and a post with TOC + mermaid (pick one from src/blog/en-us/).
2) Each screenshot taken in both light and dark modes.
3) Each screenshot taken at 3 viewports: 375 (mobile), 768 (tablet), 1440 (desktop).
4) Baselines committed under tests/e2e/__screenshots__/.
5) CI runs on PRs; fails with diff image artifacts on pixel diff > threshold (default Playwright 0.2).
6) README or CLAUDE.md updated with 'how to regenerate baselines' instructions.
notes: This guards visual regressions across all trims. Without it, TDD is numeric-only and misses styling breakage.

title: Add CSS bundle category-budget unit test (TDD red)
description: Add a Vitest unit test that reads _site/css/style.css (produced by build:prod) and asserts both total size and per-category budgets. This is the red test that every trim PR ratchets against. Categorizes rules via regex on selectors (prose, burger, preflight, feature components) so we catch 'typography grew 10KB' separately from 'everything else grew 10KB'.
design: Categorization mirrors the audit buckets (prose via ':where(' + h1/h2/p/code/pre/ul/ol/li/etc. AND .prose prefix; feature components via class-name prefix). Unit test, not e2e, so it runs in <1s. Thresholds start at current-observed + small headroom so initial state is green; each trim PR lowers the relevant threshold.
acceptance_criteria:
1) tests/unit/css/bundle-size.test.js exists.
2) Test reads _site/css/style.css; fails clearly if file missing with hint to run 'npm run build:prod'.
3) Asserts total < configured limit from package.json bundleSizeLimit.css.
4) Asserts per-category budgets: typography-prose < 35000, burger-menu < 6000, preflight/base < 5000, feature-components (dictionary+theme-toggle+language+reading-progress+toc+mermaid+back-to-top+link-components) < 15000, other-utilities < 10000.
5) CI runs it (already covered by 'npm test' in test.yml after build:prod).
6) Test is deterministic: same bytes in → same categorization.
7) Documented: when a trim lands, update the thresholds in the same PR.
notes: Regression coverage: prevents silent prose bloat, silent feature-component bloat, AND silent net growth. Replaces the single-number check in test.yml as the authoritative guard; test.yml check stays as a cheap fast-fail in CI shell.

title: Bundle mermaid locally (Rollup + code-splitting), remove unpkg CDN dep
description:
## Why
Mermaid diagrams stopped rendering after commit 9ac0f08 added a strict CSP. The eleventy-plugin-mermaid shortcode emits \`import mermaid from 'https://unpkg.com/mermaid@11.11.0/...'\`, but CSP script-src only allows cdn.jsdelivr.net (plus 'self' and narrow analytics hosts). Browser console shows:
> Refused to load script at https://unpkg.com/mermaid@11.11.0/dist/mermaid.esm.min.mjs from script-src-elem.

## Decision
Self-host mermaid by bundling it via Rollup (same pattern as scripts/build-prism.js). Rationale:
- ESM + SRI is impractical: mermaid's entry dynamically imports ~30 chunk files; SRI only covers the entry.
- UMD + SRI from jsdelivr works but ships a 2.7MB file with no tree-shaking.
- Rollup bundling from node_modules gives tree-shaking, code-splitting (mermaid already uses dynamic imports per diagram type), and same-origin trust — no third-party script on script-src at all.
- Site runs on GitHub Pages; "self-hosting" just means committing the build output or producing it in CI, same as build-prism.

## Acceptance
- \`npm run build\` produces \`_site/_static/js/vendor/mermaid/\` with entry + per-diagram chunks
- \`{% mermaid_js %}\` shortcode emits a \`<script type="module" src="/.../mermaid/index.js">\` pointing at the local bundle (no external origin)
- CSP unchanged or tightened (does NOT need to add unpkg)
- Existing mermaid-theme-sync.js works unchanged (it guards on \`typeof mermaid === 'undefined'\`)
- Unit tests cover: shortcode output, bundle entry emits \`window.mermaid\`, build script produces output
- E2E verified: blog post with mermaid diagram renders (curl-bash-pipe-security, mcp-security, or get-most-out-of-claude-code)
- No regressions on code-copy-buttons, dictionary, theme toggle

## Related
Parent audit: blog-6t3

title: Diceware: add Content-Security-Policy for site (H2)
description:
Adversarial review of the diceware generator flagged that no CSP is set anywhere (no _headers, netlify.toml, or meta CSP). The generator's security posture depends on no other script running on the page — any compromised or third-party script on this origin can read the generated phrase from outEl.textContent or sl-copy-button.value and exfiltrate it.

Add a strict CSP, ideally via _headers if the host supports it, or meta http-equiv as fallback. Minimum: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Tool pages under /tools/ are a good candidate for a tightened per-path policy.

Verify Shoelace (imported in head.njk) still works under the policy — it may need a style-src allowance.

title: Global solution for language switch returning to post-not-translated
description: When a user is on /<lang>/post-not-translated/ and switches language, the selector should restore the originating page in the new language. Current approach: sessionStorage tracks 'lastViewedBlogSlug' for blog posts and (new) 'lastViewedBasePath' for non-blog pages. This is a patch — we've hit this bug more than once. Design a permanent, global mechanism that works for every content type (pages, tools, blog posts, future sections) without per-type storage keys. Candidates: (a) single 'lastViewedOriginPath' key that always stores the language-stripped origin, with a known set of prefixes stripped; (b) pass the origin path as a query param on redirect to post-not-translated (eleventy-side) and read it client-side; (c) generate a static per-language set of translated paths and fall back via server-side redirect. Pick one, implement, delete the two sessionStorage keys.
close_reason: Replaced sessionStorage + HEAD-fetch with 11ty I18nPlugin locale_links (PR #128)

title: Ratchet bundleSizeLimit.css back to tight budget
description: After trim PRs land, lower package.json bundleSizeLimit.css to (current_size + 10%) and align with category budgets in the unit test. Locks in the gains.
design: Simple numeric update, gated by all trims being merged.
acceptance_criteria:
1) bundleSizeLimit.css set to round figure at (current_size + 10%), not to exceed 50000.
2) CI test.yml bundle-size shell check still passes.
3) tests/unit/css/bundle-size.test.js thresholds match.
4) Commit message references the trim PRs that enabled the ratchet.
notes: Must be the LAST PR in the epic. Run after A+C (minimum) or A+B+C+F (ideal).

title: Trim B: drop prose-lg modifier, keep base prose size
description: prose-lg duplicates the entire typography rule set at larger font sizes — ~5-7KB. Blog uses responsive html font-size already (raw-website.css 393,415,456,475), so prose-lg is largely redundant. Drop it and verify heading/paragraph sizing remains acceptable across breakpoints.
design: Risk is higher than A/C because prose-lg touches line-height, margins, list spacing, and code-block sizing — not just colors. Visual regression is the primary guard; do NOT ship if any baseline diff exceeds threshold without manual sign-off.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Remove prose-lg from @apply in raw-website.css:155.
2) Adjust any per-element sizes if needed in @layer base (already have h1/h2/h3 overrides via --header-scale-* vars; likely no change needed).
3) Bundle-size test passes with prose-budget threshold lowered by ~5KB.
4) Visual regression passes across all 3 viewports on the blog-post baseline page.
5) Manual review: read a long blog post at each breakpoint and confirm line-height, paragraph spacing, header hierarchy still look good.
notes: Highest-risk trim in the quick-win set. Land AFTER A and C.

title: Trim C: drop prose-sky modifier, inline link colors
description: prose-sky adds ~1.5KB for what amounts to link colors inside prose. Replace with two raw rules: '.prose a { color: theme(colors.sky.700) }' and hover/dark variants.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Remove 'prose-sky' from @apply in raw-website.css:155.
2) Add .prose a + :hover + html.dark .prose a + :hover rules.
3) Bundle-size test passes with prose-budget threshold lowered by ~1.5KB.
4) Visual regression passes; link colors in blog post prose match baseline within tolerance.
5) External/internal/dictionary link styling (already custom) unchanged.
notes: Trivial after Trim A lands; can be bundled in same PR if reviewer OK.

title: Trim A: replace dark:prose-p/li/lead modifiers with raw rules
description: Biggest single trim. Stacked prose modifiers in src/_tailwindCSS/raw-website.css:155 ('dark:prose-p:text-slate-50 dark:prose-li:text-slate-50 dark:prose-lead:text-slate-100 prose-lead:font-light') emit ~12-16KB of full :where() rule sets. Replace with ~6 hand-written rules in @layer base scoped to article/main.
design: The typography plugin emits :where() selectors at specificity 0-0-0 so any article p / html.dark article p raw rule (0-0-2 / 0-1-2) beats them. Place rules in @layer base so they're purge-safe.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Source diff: remove the four modifiers from the @apply line.
2) Add raw CSS rules: 'html.dark article p { color: theme(colors.slate.50); }' etc. for p, li, lead, with matching selectors so specificity wins over .prose :where().
3) Bundle-size test passes with tighter prose-budget threshold (lower by at least 10KB).
4) Playwright visual regression passes (no pixel diff > 0.2 on any baseline page).
5) Manual spot-check: blog post body text color matches main in light AND dark mode.
6) bundleSizeLimit.css updated to new observed size + 3KB headroom in same commit.
notes: TDD flow: 1) lower prose threshold in bundle-size.test.js → fails. 2) apply source change → rebuild → test passes. 3) run visual regression → passes. 4) commit + PR.

title: Inventory CDN usage and enforce secure loading across all third-party assets
description: ## Why Mermaid regression (blocked by CSP post-9ac0f08) surfaced that the site relies on several third-party CDNs with inconsistent integrity guarantees. Need a single audit + remediation plan rather than fixing each one ad-hoc. ## Scope 1. Inventory every external origin currently referenced: - `src/_includes/head.njk` CSP directives (script-src, style-src, font-src, connect-src) - All `

title: [EPIC] Audit and trim CSS bundle to restore tight budget
description:
CSS bundle has tripled from 35KB to 67KB (CI, prod) / 82KB (local, no purgecss). Direct-to-main pushes let bloat accumulate across typography plugin modifiers, feature accretion (burger, mermaid, TOC, diceware, reading-progress, back-to-top), and a broken build chain where `npm run build` skips purgecss.

This epic coordinates the audit + trim + regression-prevention work.

## Root causes (from audit)

1. **Typography plugin modifiers = 52% of bundle (34.3KB / 67KB).** `src/_tailwindCSS/raw-website.css:155` stacks `prose prose-sky dark:prose-invert dark:prose-p:text-slate-50 dark:prose-li:text-slate-50 dark:prose-lead:text-slate-100 prose-lead:font-light prose-lg`. Each modifier emits a full set of `:where(h1,...,pre,table,img,...)` rules.
2. **Feature accretion: ~15KB** across burger (5.6K), preflight (4.3K), dictionary-tooltip (2.4K), theme-toggle (2.3K), language-selector (1.7K), reading-progress (1.3K), toc/anchor (1.2K), mermaid (0.6K), back-to-top (0.6K).
3. **Build asymmetry.** `npm run build` doesn't set NODE_ENV=production so postcss-purgecss is skipped locally → 82KB dev vs 67KB CI. Devs can't reproduce CI output.
4. **Regression-prevention gap.** Size check only in `.github/workflows/test.yml`; the last commit raised the limit to 90K instead of fixing the cause.

## Strategy

TDD per trim: add budget test (red) and Playwright visual-regression baseline, then apply trims one at a time, each in its own PR, each gated by the test + visual diff.

## Success criteria for the epic

- `bundleSizeLimit.css` ratcheted back to ≤ 50KB (target ~45KB) without visual regressions on home / blog post / diceware / dictionary pages.
- `npm run build` and CI build produce identical byte-for-byte CSS.
- Category budgets (prose, feature CSS) enforced in unit tests.
- Child issues closed: blog-wyu1 through blog-wyu10.

title: Diceware: regenerate phrase on pageshow to cover bfcache restore (M1)
description:
Currently generate() is called once at init (main.js:155). On a bfcache restore (back/forward button), the module does not re-execute, so the previously generated passphrase is visible again to whoever has the tab now. On a shared machine this leaks the last-viewed phrase.

Fix: bind generate() to window 'pageshow'. It fires both on fresh load and on bfcache restore; event.persisted === true identifies the bfcache case if we ever need to distinguish. Replace the lone initial generate() call at the end of init() with a pageshow listener.

Pairs with blog-4pp (clear on hide): hide clears, show regenerates, no stale secret sticks around across tab switches or history navigation.

title: Diceware: clear generated phrase from DOM + copy-button on hide/unload (M2)
description:
Generated passphrase persists in two DOM channels until the next generate(): outEl.textContent (main.js:103) and copyBtn.value (main.js:104). Anything with DOM access — browser extensions with activeTab, screenshots, save-page-as, DevTools inspection — reads both trivially.

Clear both when the page is hidden or unloaded:
- visibilitychange -> document.hidden: clear outEl.textContent and set copyBtn.value = ''
- beforeunload / pagehide: same

Do not regenerate on show here — that is handled by the pageshow-regenerate work for M1 (see separate issue). Just clear. Consider also clearing copyBtn.value a short time after a successful copy so the secret is not resident between copy and regenerate.

title: Diceware: add fetch integrity to wordlist load (H1)
description:
The wordlist SHA-256 pin is injected into the HTML from site data (diceware.njk:42) and read back in main.js. An attacker who can tamper with the served HTML can swap both the pin and the wordlist. This is inherent to in-page verification, but we can upgrade the check to browser-enforced by passing integrity: 'sha256-<base64>' to the fetch() call in wordlist-loader.js:96.

The browser will refuse to return a body (and refuse to populate any HTTP cache) if the digest does not match. The existing crypto.subtle hash check stays as defense-in-depth (it also covers the Cache Storage read path, which integrity: does not).

Add a site-data field with the base64 form of the digest (the current hex pin stays for the subtle check). Wire it through diceware.njk as another data- attribute. Pass it into loadWordlist. Include { integrity } in the fetch options when present, guarded so the loader still works in test envs that inject fetchImpl.

title: Spike: route-scoped CSS for diceware/mermaid/TOC
description: Shoelace tooltip CSS, mermaid dark-mode overrides, TOC styling, diceware-specific rules currently ship in the site-wide bundle but are only used on specific routes. Evaluate options for per-route CSS splitting: (a) Eleventy per-page CSS generation via a build step, (b) critical CSS inlined per layout + lazy full stylesheet, (c) module-scoped stylesheet <link>s only on pages that need them.
design: This is the biggest structural win long-term but not required to hit 35KB. Park behind the quick-win trims.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Research-only spike: document the three options with pros/cons for this stack (Eleventy + Tailwind + Nunjucks).
2) Prototype option (c) for diceware on a throwaway branch, measure site-wide bundle reduction.
3) Recommendation written as bd comment; if go, file new task for implementation.

title: Research spike: evaluate Tailwind v4 migration
description: Tailwind v4 (Oxide engine) typically produces 30-60% smaller output and is faster. Spike: migrate this project in a throwaway branch, measure bundle size and build time, identify breakages (custom config, typography plugin compat, autoprefixer, purgecss, @apply usage).
design: Research only. No production change from this issue. Timebox: 1 working session.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Throwaway branch with tailwind v4 installed + config migrated.
2) Measured: bundle size delta (prod), build time delta, list of breakages.
3) Written go/no-go recommendation as bd comment on this issue.
4) If go: new task issue created for the actual migration PR. If no-go: documented reason (plugin incompatibility, @apply limits, etc.).
notes: Do AFTER the quick-win trims have landed so we measure v4 against an already-trim baseline.

title: Trim D: consolidate burger-menu media-query variants
description: burger-menu is 5.6KB / 41 rules. Most of that is three separate @media (max-width: 640px) blocks with 1-3 rules each scattered around raw-website.css (108, 261, 289, 300, 310, 345, 363, 384). Consolidate to one block; consider CSS vars to eliminate duplication.
design: Extract sizes to --burger-width, --control-size variables switched via media query once; rule bodies use the vars.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Source: burger and mobile-control CSS consolidated (single media block or CSS-variable-driven sizing).
2) Bundle-size test: burger-menu category < 4500 bytes (from ~5600).
3) Visual regression passes on mobile viewport (375w) for home + post + diceware.
4) Burger menu still opens/closes with same animation timings.
notes: Lower priority; modest savings but cleans up authoring.

title: Trim E: delete unused image-optimization classes from source
description: .image-gallery, .image-grid-2/3/4, .lightbox-trigger, .picture-fallback, .thumbnail, .hero-image, .aspect-ratio-container appear in raw-website.css (lines 681-817) but grep shows zero usages in src/**. Purgecss already strips them from prod bundle, but they bloat the source and confuse future maintainers.
design: Pure source cleanup. Zero runtime impact. Keeps the source file shorter and auditable.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Verify zero usages across src/ (grep for each class).
2) Remove the orphan classes from raw-website.css.
3) Bundle-size test passes (no change in prod bundle expected — purgecss already purged).
4) Visual regression passes (should be a no-op).

title: Trim F (spike): remove @tailwindcss/typography plugin, hand-write prose
description: The typography plugin generates ~30KB of CSS for what the blog actually uses (headings, paragraphs, lists, code, blockquote, pre, img, a, strong). Hand-write these as ~30-50 lines in @layer base and drop the plugin. Estimated savings: ~20KB net after writing replacement.
design: Highest-leverage trim but highest risk. Gate: spike must produce a visual-regression pass before committing to the full refactor. Reference: typography plugin source at https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss-typography for defaults to match.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Spike first: prototype replacement in a branch, measure final bundle size.
2) If estimate holds (< 35KB total bundle), proceed to full PR.
3) Remove @tailwindcss/typography from package.json + tailwind.config.js.
4) Add hand-written prose rules targeting article/main > :not(.not-prose).
5) All bundle-size test budgets pass with typography-prose < 10000.
6) Visual regression passes with manual sign-off on each baseline page (high bar: prose spacing is sensitive).
7) Remove the 'prose' and 'prose-*' entries from postcss.config.js safelist.
notes: Do NOT attempt until A+B+C have shipped and their savings are measured.

title: Generic breadcrumb component for non-blog pages
description: Static pages like /en-us/tools/diceware/ and /en-us/about/ render without breadcrumbs, so users who open them directly (social, search) have no obvious way back. Design a reusable breadcrumb that derives segments from page.url, translates segment labels via the i18n filter (fall back to the raw slug when no translation exists), and renders in the page layout by default. Accessibility: nav[aria-label='Breadcrumb'] with an ordered list. Skip on home/404/post-not-translated.

title: Translate diceware tool page to el and tr
description: src/pages/tools/diceware.njk is English-only with a permalink of /en-us/tools/diceware/. Paginate over supportedLocales like src/pages/security.md does, so /el/tools/diceware/ and /tr/tools/diceware/ exist. Translate all prose (intro, section headers, entropy labels, crack-time table headings, anchor-phrase prefix) and confirm the UI labels inside the shoelace controls. Once translations land, the language switcher will auto-route correctly and we can drop any English-only hinting.

value: To read a memory, use `bd memories <keyword>`, NOT `bd remember <key>`. Running `bd remember <key>` without content silently overwrites the memory body with just the key string. To update, always pass explicit content: `bd remember --key <key> "<new content>"`. Why: clobbered the blog-always-open-prs memory body twice this session by mistyping the read-vs-write command. How to apply: when wanting to inspect a memory, only ever use `bd memories` (search) or `bd show <key>` — never `bd remember` in a read context.

On the blog repo, ALWAYS open a PR — never push directly to main, even if branch protection allows bypass. Why: CI runs on PRs and the user wants visible review history. How to apply: for any blog change, create a branch, push, and open a PR via gh — do not push to main even for small fixes.
